states.text: The guard blocks the path, hand resting on a weapon.
The guard steps aside. "Move along."
"Wolves have been raiding caravans on the north road. Watch yourself out there."
The merchant spreads their hands over the wares. "See anything you like?"
"I've got supplies for the road, weapons for the bold, and a few curiosities besides."
"Alright, alright. I'll knock a bit off the price for you. But don't tell anyone."
"They say the old mine south of town has been overrun. Goblins, maybe worse."
"Brave soul. Bring back proof and I'll make it worth your while."
The innkeeper wipes down the bar. "Welcome, traveler. What'll it be?"

transitions.text: Rent a room for the night
Do you have supplies for the road?

states.text: "Room's upstairs, second door on the left. Rest well."
"A strange fellow passed through last week. Asked about the ruins up north. Nobody goes there anymore."
"I've got dried rations and a few torches. Nothing fancy, but it'll keep you going."
Sparks fly as the blacksmith sets down the hammer. "Need something made or mended?"
"Iron swords, axes, a few spearheads. All solid work."
"Bring me the piece and I'll see what I can do. Won't be cheap, mind."
"You've got the materials. I can work with that. What did you have in mind?"
The healer looks up from a mortar of herbs. "You look like you could use some tending."
"There. The herbs will do their work. Rest if you can."
"I have a few remedies. Healing salves, antidotes, and a rare restorative."
"Bring the item here. My knowledge of the old texts may reveal its nature."
A shadowy figure leans against the alley wall, flipping a coin. "Looking for trouble, or just lost?"
"Maybe. The merchant guild's got a shipment coming in. Could use a distraction."
"Smart choice. Meet me here at midnight. Don't be late."
The rogue raises their hands. "Easy, friend. No need for violence."
You deftly slip a few coins from the rogue's pouch. They don't seem to notice.
A weary villager glances up. "Can I help you with something?"
"The harvest's been poor, and folks are worried about bandits on the road."
"Thank you! Not many lend a hand these days. Here, take this."
"I did see something shiny by the well. Maybe check there?"
A sinister figure sits atop a crude throne. "So, you've found me at last."
"You think words will sway me? Prove your worth, then!"
The villain narrows their eyes. "Perhaps we can come to an arrangement... for now."
The villain draws a wicked blade. "Let's end this."
A robed figure beckons you over. "You look capable. Are you seeking work?"
"Bandits have been harassing travelers near the old bridge. Deal with them, and you'll be rewarded."
"Return when the road is safe. The townsfolk will thank you."
The mayor sits behind a heavy oak desk, poring over documents. "Ah, the newcomer. I've heard of your arrival."

transitions.text: Is there something I can do for the town?
Tell me about the local factions
I won't take more of your time.

states.text: "Supplies run thin and morale is slipping. The north road is barely passable, and our last courier never returned."
"Our supply caravan is three days overdue. I fear the worst. Find the caravan and bring back whatever you can salvage."

params.text: The mayor asked me to find the missing supply caravan on the north road.

transitions.journal_entry: Accepted the mayor's request to locate the missing supply caravan.

states.text: "You have the town's gratitude. Speak to the guard captain for details on the route."
"The merchant guild holds the coin, the temple holds the faith, and the guard holds the peace. Balance is fragile."
The priest turns from the altar, hands clasped. "Welcome, child. Do you seek solace or purpose?"

transitions.text: I heard there are restless dead in the old cemetery.

states.text: "May the light guide your path through the darkness ahead."
"It is true. The dead do not rest. Something profane stirs beneath the old stones. Will you cleanse this blight?"

transitions.text: I will put them to rest.

params.text: The priest gave me holy water and asked me to cleanse the cemetery of restless dead.

transitions.journal_entry: Accepted the priest's request to cleanse the old cemetery.

states.text: "Take this holy water. It will burn the unclean. Return when the ground is sanctified."
"We serve the Light of Dawn. It demands compassion, vigilance, and the courage to face what festers in shadow."

transitions.text: Interesting. What else can you tell me?

states.text: A bard strums an idle tune in the corner. "Another wanderer! Sit down, I've got stories to trade."

transitions.text: Tell me a story about this place.

transitions.journal_entry: Reported the caravan's fate. The bard rewarded me on behalf of the town.

states.text: "They say the founders buried a sealed vault beneath the town square. No one's found the key in a hundred years."
"A hermit in the eastern woods claims to have found something ancient. He trades knowledge for rare herbs."
"Well done! The town owes you a debt. Here's what the council put together."
The scribe peers over spectacles at a stack of ledgers. "Yes? I have little time for idle talk."

transitions.text: I need access to the town records.
Has anything unusual turned up in the archives?
The cemetery is cleansed. Here is proof.
transitions.journal_entry: The scribe verified the cemetery cleansing and recorded the deed in the town archives.

states.text: "The records are open to citizens. For outsiders, you'll need the mayor's writ. What are you looking for?"
"A sealed page from a century-old journal surfaced during reorganization. It mentions a ritual site in the hills. Someone should investigate."
"Excellent. The deed is recorded. The temple sends its thanks, and here is a scroll for your trouble."
An elderly sage sits surrounded by open tomes and strange instruments. "Knowledge is never free. What do you seek?"

transitions.text: Tell me about the old ruins.
Can you identify a magical artifact? [MND 12]
Do you need help with any research?

states.text: "The ruins north of here predate the current kingdom by three centuries. Built by a people who mastered wards of binding. Be cautious inside."
"Fascinating. This bears the mark of the Binding Order. Hold onto it carefully."
"I need rare reagents from the marshlands south of the settlement. Swamp moss and cave lichen. Bring them back and I'll share what I've learned about the sealed vault."

params.text: The sage needs swamp moss and cave lichen from the southern marshlands.

transitions.journal_entry: Accepted the sage's request to gather rare reagents.

states.text: "Good. Be warned: the marshlands are not safe after dark."
A weathered woman stirs a bubbling pot in a cluttered cottage. "Come in, come in. I knew you'd find me."

transitions.text: I've heard there's a curse on the old mill.
I've brought the reagents the sage asked for.
transitions.journal_entry: Delivered the reagents. The witch paid me for the trouble.

states.text: "I deal in remedies most alchemists won't touch. Antidotes, night-vision draughts, and a tonic that sharpens the mind."
"The mill curse is real. An old grudge between two families, sealed with blood magic. It can be lifted, but you'll need a silver mirror and a willing heart."
"Perfect. These will do nicely. The sage will be pleased. Here's something for your trouble."
A guard on patrol stops and eyes you warily. "State your business. These streets aren't safe after dark."

transitions.text: What kind of trouble have you seen?
I want to report suspicious activity.

params.entry: A patrol guard told you to report suspicious movement to the garrison captain before dusk.

states.text: "Pickpockets near the market, smugglers down by the docks, and something foul lurking in the sewers. Take your pick of troubles."
"Report it to the captain at the garrison. I'm just a foot patrol, but I'll keep an eye out."
The prison guard leans on his halberd beside iron-banded doors. "No visitors unless you've got authorization from the magistrate."
The guard steps aside and unlocks the heavy door. "Make it quick. And don't cause any trouble."
"A smuggler, a horse thief, and one they dragged in last week raving about the dead walking. The usual lot."
A weathered harbor guard watches ships come and go. "The docks are restricted past sundown. What brings you here?"

transitions.text: I've heard there are smugglers operating here.

params.entry: The harbor guard hinted at smuggler activity near warehouse seven.

states.text: "A trading galley from the south, a fishing fleet, and one vessel with no flag that arrived in the night. That last one has the captain uneasy."
"Keep your voice down. Warehouse seven, after midnight. That's all I'll say. Don't mention my name."
Two guards flank a barricade on the road. "Halt. The pass is closed due to bandit activity. Turn back."

transitions.text: I can deal with the bandits. Where are they?

params.entry: Checkpoint guards reported bandits in the ravine beyond the pass.

states.text: "Military business. Go ahead, but watch your back out there. Last patrol didn't come back."
"They've dug in at the ravine beyond the next ridge. A dozen strong, maybe more. Clear them out and report back for a bounty."
A guard captain stands before a board covered in wanted notices. "Looking to earn some coin? We've got bounties."

transitions.text: I've dealt with the bandits at the ravine.

states.text: "Wolves harassing the north road, bandits in the ravine, and a fugitive alchemist last seen near the marshes. Pick your poison."
"Confirmed. The road is safer because of you. Here's your pay, and the guard remembers those who serve."
A guard sergeant is assembling a small escort detail. "We need someone with a blade to help move a prisoner transport. Interested?"
"South gate to the fortress at Helm's Reach. Three days on the road, through wolf territory. We pay twenty gold on arrival."
"Good. Gear up and meet us at the south gate by dawn. Don't be late."
A guard peers down from the watchtower. "You shouldn't be up here. What do you want?"

transitions.text: Seen anything unusual from up here?
I've spotted something large in the sky to the east.

states.text: "Smoke rising from the old watchtower east of here, and movement in the tree line that doesn't look like deer. Something is stirring out there."
The guard's face goes pale. "A dragon? I'll sound the alarm. Get to the captain immediately. Go!"
A merchant in colorful robes gestures to crates of foreign goods. "From the far shores! Spices, silks, and curiosities you won't find elsewhere."

transitions.text: Where do your goods come from?

states.text: "Firebloom tea from the southern isles, a compass that points to the nearest water, and a blade of eastern steel. All at fair prices."
"The southern sea route, when it's open. Lately pirates have been a problem. If someone cleared that lane, profits would soar."

transitions.text: I see. Good luck with business.

states.text: A jeweler examines a gemstone through a loupe. "Ah, a discerning eye approaches. Looking for something precious?"

transitions.text: Can you appraise something for me? [INS 12]

states.text: "Ruby pendants, sapphire rings, and a rare moonstone amulet said to ward off curses. The moonstone alone is worth a small fortune."
"Hmm, interesting craftsmanship. This is worth more than it appears. Hold onto it, or I'll give you forty gold for it right now."
Bundles of dried herbs hang from the ceiling. The herbalist nods. "Need a remedy, or gathering supplies?"

transitions.text: Do you need any rare ingredients gathered?

states.text: "Dried elfroot, crushed nightshade, thornberry extract, and a few bundles of healwort. All freshly prepared."
"Frostcap mushrooms only grow in the high caves. Bring me five and I'll pay generously."
"Wonderful. The caves are a day's trek northeast. Watch for bears."
A bespectacled shopkeeper arranges leather-bound volumes. "A reader! How delightful. Most folk here can barely sign their name."

transitions.text: What do you have in stock?

states.text: "Histories of the realm, a few bestiaries, and one grimoire I'm not entirely comfortable selling. But for the right price..."
"You have a scholar's bearing. I may have something in the back. A treatise on the Binding Order, predating the current texts. Fifty gold."
Racks of chainmail and leather armor line the walls. The armorer crosses thick arms. "You look like you need better protection."
"Leather jerkins for the light on their feet, chainmail for those who can bear the weight, and one brigandine set that's seen real battle."
"You've got the build for heavy plate. Bring me iron bars and I'll forge something worthy of a knight. Thirty gold for the labor."

params.entry: The armorer will forge fitted plate if you return with iron and enough coin.

states.text: A plump vendor tends a stall of bread, cheese, and dried meats. "Fresh today! Well, fresh enough. What'll you have?"
"Five bundles of hardtack and jerky. Won't win any feasts, but it'll keep you walking."
"The farmers south of town say something's been poisoning their well. Livestock dropping dead, crops withering. Dark business."
A fletcher tests the draw on a shortbow. "Arrows, bows, crossbow bolts. If it flies and sticks, I make it."
"Iron-tipped arrows by the quiver, broadheads for hunting, and flight arrows for range. All hand-fletched."
"Fire arrows, if you supply the pitch. And I've been experimenting with barbed tips that are murder to pull out. Ten gold per quiver."
The innkeeper of this dingy establishment scratches a scar on his chin. "Room's two gold. No questions asked, none answered."

transitions.text: I'm looking for work. The kind that doesn't involve the guard.

states.text: "End of the hall. Lock your door. I'd suggest sleeping with one eye open."
"There's a man in the back corner. Goes by Whisper. Don't stare, don't ask his real name. He's got connections."
A well-dressed innkeeper stands behind a polished mahogany bar. "Welcome to the Silver Chalice. We cater to those of refined taste."
"The Ambassador Suite. Feather bed, private bath, and a view of the gardens. Breakfast is included."
"Lord Varen is here on trade business, and a scholar from the capital arrived yesterday. Both keep to themselves, which suits us fine."
A tired woman runs a small wayside inn. "You're the first traveler in three days. The road must be bad. Stew's on the fire."

transitions.text: I'll have some stew and a bed.

states.text: "Room's upstairs. Simple but clean. You look like you need the rest."
"Washed out bridge two leagues east. You'll have to ford the river or take the mountain path. Neither is pleasant."
A burly innkeeper tends a roaring fireplace in a stone-walled lodge. "Come in from the cold. We've got mead and warmth."

transitions.text: A room and something hot to drink.

states.text: "Warm furs and a strong fire. You'll sleep well tonight, provided the wind doesn't howl too loud."
"Snowed shut two weeks ago. The dwarven miners say there's a tunnel route, but they charge for passage. Talk to the foreman at the mine entrance."
A master smith works at a massive forge, her arms scarred by decades of labor. "I don't make trinkets. Bring me a worthy commission or don't waste my time."

transitions.text: I can gather rare materials for you.
I'll come back when I'm ready.

states.text: "You've got the build to wield something great. Bring me star iron from the fallen stone near the old battlefield and I'll forge a blade like no other."

params.entry: The master smith needs star iron from the fallen stone near the old battlefield.

transitions.text: That sounds like a long journey.

states.text: "If you're serious, I need star iron. A meteorite fell near the old battlefield years ago. Dig it out and bring it here."
A stout dwarven smith hammers at a glowing ingot. "Surface-dweller. You want dwarven steel? It costs dwarven gold."

transitions.text: Tell me about dwarven smithing traditions.

states.text: "War hammers that never dull, axes balanced to perfection, and a shield that's turned aside dragon flame. Every piece is a legacy."
"We forge in the deep fires where the earth's blood runs hot. Each piece is sung into shape over seven days. No shortcuts, no half-measures."
An elven artisan shapes a slender blade with deliberate, almost meditative strokes. "You seek something of elven make? Patience is the first requirement."

transitions.text: I'd like to commission a piece. [PRE 13]

states.text: "Blades lighter than a feather yet stronger than common steel. Bows carved from heartwood that sings in the wind. Each piece takes a season to complete."
"You carry yourself with some grace. I will accept your commission. Return in a fortnight with sixty gold and the work will be done."
A gentle herbalist tends rows of potted plants in a sun-dappled greenhouse. "Nature provides all the medicine we need. How can I help?"

transitions.text: I've been poisoned. Can you help?

states.text: "Poultices for wounds, tea for fever, and a tincture that eases pain. All made from what grows in these hills."
"Drink this. It will taste foul, but the poison will pass within the hour. Rest and drink water."
A robed healer kneels in prayer, hands glowing faintly with golden light. "The Light of Dawn heals all who ask with honest hearts. Come forward."
Light washes over you, and old wounds knit shut. "Go in peace, and carry the light's warmth with you."
"We channel the light through faith and discipline. It is not our power, but a gift we are entrusted to share."
A haggard healer works frantically among rows of sick villagers. "Another pair of hands. Thank the heavens. Can you help?"
"The well water is tainted. Something upstream is poisoning the supply. Find the source and stop it, or this village is finished."

transitions.text: I'll find what's poisoning the water.

params.entry: The plague healer says the well water is poisoned from upstream. I need to find the source.

transitions.text: I'm sorry. I can't help right now.

states.text: "Follow the stream north. Whatever you find, be careful. This isn't natural sickness."

transitions.text: I'll get to the bottom of it.

states.text: "No disease I've seen before. It acts like poison but spreads like fever. Something dark is at work."
A well-dressed man sits behind a hidden counter in a back room. "Goods to move? I don't ask where they came from, you don't ask where they go."

transitions.text: I need information about the smuggler network.

states.text: "I'll give you sixty percent of market value. That's generous for no-questions merchandise. Take it or leave it."
"Information costs. Twenty gold and I'll point you in the right direction. Warehouse district, after dark. Ask for Red Veil."
A nervous figure in a hooded cloak darts glances around the alley. "You the one asking about the spy network? Keep your voice down."

transitions.text: What do you know about the spy ring?
Talk, or I make you talk. [MIG 13]

states.text: "Someone in the mayor's office is feeding information to the warlord in the east. They use coded messages hidden in trade manifests. That's all I know."

params.entry: An informant revealed a spy in the mayor's office passing coded messages to the eastern warlord.

states.text: "Alright, alright! The spy uses a dead drop in the market fountain. Check there at noon. Now let me go!"
A street urchin bumps into you, hands suspiciously quick. "Oh, sorry mister! Didn't see you there!"

transitions.text: Hold on. I could use someone with your skills.

states.text: The urchin squirms. "Let go! Fine, here's your purse back. And... these. I didn't steal much, I swear."

transitions.text: Don't let me catch you again.

states.text: "You want me to work for you? I know every back alley and sewer tunnel in this city. For a fair cut, I'm yours."
A figure draped in dark leather sits perfectly still in the shadows. Their voice is barely a whisper. "You sought the Crimson Veil. Speak your purpose."

transitions.text: I need information about a target.
I'm here to warn you. The guard knows about your guild.

states.text: "We deal in silence, not information. But if your coin is good, perhaps an arrangement can be made. Fifty gold buys you a name and a location."
"Interesting. And why would you help us? No matter. If this is true, the guild owes you a favor. Remember that."
A weathered sailor leans against stacked crates in a torchlit cellar. "Red Veil sent you? Good. We've got product to move and too few runners."

transitions.text: Actually, I'm here to shut you down.

states.text: "A crate of eastern spice needs to reach the inn at Crossroads before dawn. Avoid the patrol routes. Fifty gold on delivery."
The smuggler's hand moves to a concealed blade. "Bad move, friend. Boys, we've got a problem."
A sun-weathered farmer leans on a hoe, surveying withered crops. "Not much to see here. The blight's taken most of the season."
"Started after that strange mist rolled in from the marshes. The old-timers say it's a curse. I just know my family's going hungry."
"Bless you. Here, take some of what we salvaged. It's not much but you've earned it."
An old fisherman mends nets on the dock, pipe clenched between his teeth. "Fish aren't biting like they used to. Something in the water, I reckon."

transitions.text: What do you mean, something in the water?

states.text: "Pulled up a dead fish covered in black sores last week. And the water near the cave outlet has turned foul. Something upstream is poisoning the river."
"Three of the day's catch. Best I can offer in these lean times."
A soot-covered miner emerges from a shaft, blinking in the daylight. "The deep tunnels are off limits. Foreman's orders."

transitions.text: Why are the deep tunnels closed?
Need an extra pair of hands?

states.text: "Miners have been hearing things. Scraping, chittering sounds deep in the rock. Two men went to investigate and didn't come back. Now nobody will go past the third level."

params.entry: Miners report strange sounds and missing men in the deep tunnels.

states.text: "Surface work, sure. Hauling ore and shoring timbers. Five gold a day. Talk to the foreman if you're serious."
An elderly villager sits on a bench, watching the world go by with tired eyes. "I've lived in this village sixty years. Seen good times and bad. These are the bad times."

transitions.text: Tell me about the village's history.

params.entry: An elder warned that buried evils under the village ruins are stirring again.

transitions.text: Do you have any advice for a traveler?

states.text: "Founded by settlers fleeing the old wars. Built over ruins even older. Some say the ground here has a memory. Bad things buried don't always stay buried."
"Trust the temple folk more than the merchants. Don't wander the ruins at night. And never, ever drink from the old well at the crossroads."
A hollow-eyed refugee clutches a bundle of meager belongings. "Please, we've come from the east. The warlord's army burned our village. We have nothing."
"Thank you. You're the first person here who's shown us kindness. May the gods remember your generosity."
"He calls himself Karath the Unbroken. Commands an army of sellswords and conscripts. His camp is in the Iron Pass, east of the mountains. He won't stop with our village."
A gaunt figure stands amid flickering candles and open grimoires. The air reeks of death. "Ah, a living visitor. How refreshing."

transitions.text: Perhaps we can make a deal. [PRE 14]

states.text: The necromancer raises skeletal hands and the dead begin to stir around you. "You should have bargained when you had the chance."
"Interesting. You have more sense than the last adventurer. I'll withdraw my undead from the village. In exchange, you bring me the Binding Tome from the old archives. Do we have an accord?"
A scarred warlord sharpens a massive blade on a whetstone, surrounded by battle-hardened guards. "You've got nerve coming into my camp. State your business before I lose patience."

transitions.text: I'm here to stop your raids. [MIG 15]
The town will pay tribute for peace. [PRE 14]
I've delivered my message. I'll go.

states.text: The warlord stands, towering over you. "Bold words. Let's see if your sword backs them up. A duel. You and me. Winner takes all."
"Tribute, eh? One hundred gold a month and I'll leave your town alone. For now. Miss a payment and I burn it to the ground."
A robed cultist stands before a dark altar, arcane symbols glowing on the floor. "You've arrived just in time for the ritual. The Outer Dark hungers."
"Fool! The ritual cannot be stopped. Rise, servants of shadow!"
"The Outer Dark is the void between worlds. It promises power beyond mortal understanding. Join us, and you'll never know weakness again."

transitions.text: Tell me more about this power...

states.text: A nobleman in silk robes sips wine in a lavish study. "I know why you're here. Everyone wants something from Lord Castellan. The question is, what do you offer in return?"

transitions.text: I know you're funding the bandits.

states.text: "Funding bandits? Absurd allegations. Without proof, you're merely a nuisance. And nuisances have a way of disappearing in this city."
"Do you now? I'm listening. But know that I reward loyal associates generously, and deal harshly with those who betray my trust."
The noble's composure shatters. "Where did you... Guards! Seize this intruder!"
A grizzled bounty master reviews parchments at a worn desk. "Another hunter? Good. The board's full and the bounties are piling up."

transitions.text: I'll take the wolf den contract.

params.entry: Accepted the bounty to clear the wolf den on the north road.

states.text: "The den is a league north, in the ravine past the old bridge. Bring back pelts as proof. Twenty gold per head."
"A haunted mine needs clearing, bandits on the south road, and a missing merchant's son. All pay well."
A cloaked figure waits beneath a dim lantern. "I've been watching you. You have the look of someone who can handle... delicate matters."

transitions.text: I don't work with strangers in the dark.

states.text: "An artifact was stolen from the temple vault. The priests are keeping it quiet to avoid panic. Find it, and the reward will be substantial."

params.entry: A mysterious figure hired me to recover an artifact stolen from the temple vault.

transitions.text: Sounds like trouble I don't need.

states.text: "Start with the temple night guard. He saw something but won't talk to the priests. Here's fifty gold up front for expenses."
A frantic woman grabs your arm. "Please! My child wandered into the forest two days ago. The guards say they can't spare men. You have to help!"

params.entry: A desperate mother says her child is lost in the forest.

states.text: "The old path toward the eastern woods. By the stone marker where the road forks. Please hurry, there are wolves out there!"

transitions.text: Try the temple. They may help.

states.text: "Thank you! Thank you! I'll wait here. Please bring my little one back safe."
A wealthy merchant prince gestures from behind a gilded desk. "I have a proposition for someone with your particular skill set. The pay is excellent."
"The north trade route has been cut off by something in the forest. My caravans can't get through. Clear whatever is blocking the way and I'll make it very much worth your while."
"Excellent. My scouts report trouble near the forest crossing two leagues north. Return with good news and there will be a bonus."
A temple elder approaches with solemn urgency. "The temple has been desecrated. We need outside help to restore it, as our acolytes are too shaken to act."

transitions.text: I will help restore the temple.
I'm not the right person for this.

states.text: "Something dark entered the inner sanctum. The sacred flame was extinguished and profane symbols were carved into the altar. The acolytes who witnessed it won't speak."
"Take this holy water. You'll need it to cleanse the inner sanctum. The temple's blessings go with you."
The mayor nervously shuffles papers as you enter. "Ah, yes. The adventurer. I trust everything in town meets your satisfaction?"

transitions.text: I know about your deal with the bandits. [INS 13]
Everything is fine. Just checking in.

states.text: "I... I don't know what you're talking about. But if someone were in such a situation, hypothetically, they'd need help getting out of it. Quietly."

transitions.text: I might be able to help. For a price.
I'm taking this to the guard captain.

states.text: "Good, good. Well, if you need anything, my door is always open. Within reason."
The mayor paces behind barricaded windows. "The warlord's forces are a day's march away. We have too few soldiers and no reinforcements coming."
"We have the walls and the chokepoint at the north bridge. If someone could rally the miners and arm the able-bodied men, we might hold out."
"The south road is still clear. Women, children, and the elderly first. But we need someone to hold the rear guard."

transitions.text: Start moving people. I'll help where I can.

states.text: A pragmatic administrator reviews ledgers at a frontier trading post. "Welcome. We're a small operation here, but growing. Every traveler helps."

transitions.text: Any problems I can help with?

states.text: "We need iron and timber. Anyone who can establish a supply line with the mountain settlements would make a tidy profit."
"Wild creatures have been getting bolder. Lost two cows last week to something big. If you're handy with a weapon, the bounty is fifteen gold."
A wild-eyed priest paces before a congregation of fervent followers. "The end times approach! Only the faithful will be spared!"
The priest falters for a moment. "You... you don't understand. I've seen the signs. The darkness in the ruins, the dead walking. It's all connected."

transitions.text: Then tell me what you've seen. Calmly.

params.entry: The zealot priest claims the undead activity and the ruins are connected to a larger threat.

states.text: "The old texts speak of a seal beneath the ruins. When it breaks, the Outer Dark floods in. The seal is cracking. I can feel it."
A kindly priest tends to the sick in a makeshift ward. "Another soul in need? Step forward. The Light refuses no one."

transitions.text: I'm fine. Carry on your good work.

states.text: Warm light flows from the priest's hands. "There. May the Light keep you well on your journey."
"Fever, malnutrition, and wounds from the road. Some bear marks I can't explain. Dark veins under the skin, as if poison were crawling through them."

transitions.text: That sounds like more than natural illness.

states.text: A scholarly priest sits in a library alcove, surrounded by ancient scrolls. "Ah, a fellow seeker of truth? The temple library is at your disposal."

transitions.text: I need information about the ancient ruins.
Are there any lost texts you'd like recovered?
I'll return when I have time to read.

states.text: "The ruins were built by the Order of the Binding. They imprisoned something beneath the earth. Our oldest texts refer to it only as the Sleeper."

transitions.text: The Sleeper. I'll keep that in mind.

params.entry: The temple scholar says the ruins were built by the Order of the Binding to imprison something called the Sleeper.

states.text: "Three scrolls of warding were taken from this library decades ago. If you find them in the ruins or on the black market, I'd pay handsomely for their return."

transitions.text: Old scrolls aren't really my thing.

states.text: A dusty priest with a walking staff and sun-faded robes sits by the roadside. "The road is long, but the Light walks with us. Rest a moment, traveler."

transitions.text: A moment's rest would be welcome.

states.text: "Eat, drink. And take this blessing with you. May your blade strike true and your heart stay steadfast."
"I passed through the eastern villages. Many are empty now, the people fled. Whatever drives them west, they won't speak of it."
A bard with quick eyes and a charming smile tunes a lute by the hearth. "Songs, stories, and secrets. Everyone tells a bard things they shouldn't."

transitions.text: I need information about someone specific.

states.text: "The mayor's secretary meets with a hooded figure every third night. And the merchant guild is stockpiling weapons, not grain. Make of that what you will."
"Information has a price. Thirty gold and a name, and I'll have what you need within a week."
An animated storyteller holds a crowd of villagers spellbound with dramatic gestures. "Ah, a new face! Come, listen to the tale of the Binding and the Sleeper!"

transitions.text: Do you know any other legends?

states.text: "Long ago, the Order of the Binding imprisoned a being of terrible power beneath the earth. They built the ruins above as a ward. But wards decay, and the Sleeper stirs..."
"The founding of the town, the dragon that once roosted on the peak, and the ghost ship that sails the bay on moonless nights. Pick your favorite."
A bard sits alone, nursing a drink and plucking a mournful melody. "Every song I sing is for someone who's gone. Pull up a chair if you can bear the sorrow."

transitions.text: I'll leave you to your music.

states.text: "A friend. We traveled together for years. Lost him in the ruins when something down there woke up. I barely made it out. He didn't."
The bard manages a small smile. "You've got a way about you. Fine. One lively tune. For the living, then."
A traveling bard with a worn pack and a lute slung over one shoulder hails you on the road. "Well met! Nothing like a fellow traveler to share the miles."
"I passed through a village where they say a dragon was sighted near the eastern peaks. Could be rumor, could be trouble. Either way, it makes for a good song."
"Clear for a day's walk, then the road splits. North to the ruins, east to the mountain pass. I'd avoid the ruins if I were you. Bad energy."
A meticulous scribe works by candlelight in a cramped back room. "Official documents, letters of introduction, or... other things. What do you need?"

transitions.text: I need travel papers. No questions.

states.text: "Done. These will pass any inspection short of a royal seal. Pleasure doing business."
"I don't discuss my clients. But I'll say this: you'd be surprised how many respectable people need papers they shouldn't have."
An elderly scribe catalogs ancient texts with reverent care. "These documents are irreplaceable. Please, keep your distance from the originals."

transitions.text: I'm researching the Order of the Binding.
I found this old document in the ruins.

states.text: "The Order sealed something beneath the ruins using seven wards. Our records suggest three of those wards have already failed. The remaining four may not hold much longer."

transitions.text: How do we reinforce the wards?

params.entry: The historian says three of the seven binding wards have failed. The remaining four may not hold.

states.text: "Remarkable! This fills a gap in our records. You've done scholarship a great service. Here is compensation for your trouble."
A prim court scribe arranges quills and ink at a desk outside the council chamber. "All petitions must be submitted in writing. Do you have business with the council?"

transitions.text: I wish to address the council.
What's the council discussing these days?

states.text: "The filing fee is five gold. The council meets at week's end. Your petition will be reviewed in the order received."
"The usual. Trade disputes, land claims, and a heated debate about whether to fund the guard expansion or the temple repairs. The mayor's been unusually quiet."
A wild-haired hermit peers from a cave cluttered with trinkets and bones. "You found me. That means the forest wanted you here. Speak."

transitions.text: I seek knowledge about the ancient ruins.
I have rare herbs to trade.

states.text: "The ruins breathe. I've felt it. Something beneath is waking, and the wards are failing. The Order's work is undone. You'll need a ward stone to enter safely."

transitions.text: Where do I find a ward stone?

params.entry: The hermit sage says I need a ward stone to safely enter the ancient ruins.

states.text: "Good herbs. Here, take this ward stone. You'll need it more than I will. The ruins won't let you in without one."
A sage bent over bubbling alchemical apparatus adjusts a valve. "Careful! Don't touch anything. One wrong step and we both dissolve."

transitions.text: Can you analyze this poison sample? [MND 12]

states.text: "Healing draughts, fire resistance elixirs, and one experimental potion that might grant temporary invisibility. Emphasis on might."
"Interesting compound. This isn't natural. Someone crafted this deliberately. The base is nightshade, but there's an alchemical catalyst I've only seen in forbidden texts. This is assassin's work."

params.entry: The alchemist confirmed the poison is artificially crafted with forbidden alchemical methods.

states.text: An astronomer stands atop a tower, studying the sky through a brass telescope. "The stars have been wrong for three nights. Something is shifting in the celestial order."

transitions.text: What do the stars tell you?
Have you seen anything unusual in the sky? Like a dragon?

states.text: "The constellation of the Seal is dimming. According to the old star charts, that only happens when the binding beneath the ruins weakens. The ancient wards are connected to the stars themselves."

params.entry: The astronomer says the Seal constellation is dimming, which corresponds to weakening binding wards.

states.text: "I saw it two nights ago. A shape against the moon, heading east toward the peaks. If that's a dragon, it's the first in a century. The old roost at Iron Peak may be occupied again."

transitions.text: A dragon at Iron Peak. Noted.

states.text: A sage camps at the edge of the ancient ruins, notebooks spread before her. "These ruins are more than stone. They're a lock. And something is trying to open it from the inside."

transitions.text: I have a ward stone. Will it help?

states.text: "Seven binding wards held the seal. Three have broken. The remaining four pulse weakly. I estimate weeks, not months, before they fail entirely."

transitions.text: What happens when they all fail?

states.text: "With this I can reinforce one ward. It buys time, but not a solution. Someone needs to go below and restore the seal at its source."
A hunched witch stirs a cauldron in a hut built on stilts above fetid swamp water. "The swamp tells me things. It told me you were coming."

transitions.text: What does the swamp know about the poison in the wells?
I need a powerful brew. What's your price?

states.text: "Something unnatural seeps from the cave to the north. Not natural decay. Alchemical waste, dumped deliberately. Someone is poisoning the water on purpose."
"Three nightbloom flowers from the ruins, picked under moonlight. Bring them and I'll brew something that will make you invisible to the undead."
A reclusive witch peers from behind a beaded curtain in a mountain cave. Crystals hang from the ceiling, catching firelight. "Few climb this far. You must want something badly."

transitions.text: I seek answers about what lies beneath the ruins.

states.text: "The cards show a chained figure in darkness, and a key turning. The Sleeper stirs. You are the key, or you are the one who stops it from turning. Choose wisely."
"A charm against the evil eye, a talisman of warding, and a lodestone that hums near hidden doors. Twenty gold each."
A calm, sharp-eyed woman sits amid jars of strange ingredients. "Curses are my specialty. Laying them, breaking them, or teaching you about them. Which do you need?"
"Hold still. This will sting." She draws a symbol on your forehead with cold silver ink. The weight lifts. "Done. Whatever was clinging to you is gone."
"Every curse has three parts: the intent, the anchor, and the fuel. Break any one and the curse unravels. Most amateurs forget about the anchor. That's usually an object tied to the victim."
A young witch with an eager smile tends a stall of colorful bottles. "Potions for every occasion! Love, luck, and a few that the apothecary guild would rather I didn't sell."

transitions.text: What about the ones they don't want you selling?

states.text: "Healing tonics, stamina draughts, antidotes, and a potion that lets you see in the dark for an hour. All reasonably priced."
"Whisper it. I have a truth serum, a sleeping draught that works on anything, and one flask of liquid fire. That last one is fifty gold and you didn't get it from me."
A ranger in worn leathers cleans a bow at a woodland outpost. "You're off the main road. Looking for something, or just lost?"

transitions.text: What can you tell me about this area?

states.text: "Wolf packs to the north, the old ruins to the east, and something has been spooking the wildlife south of here. The forest isn't safe anymore."
"Actually, yes. A wolf pack has settled near the north road and they're growing bolder. Clear the den and the outpost will owe you."
A ranger crouches over tracks in the mud, muttering to herself. "These prints are wrong. Too large for a wolf, too deep for a bear. Something else is out there."

transitions.text: What do you think made those tracks?
I'm a fair tracker myself. Can I help? [INS 12]

states.text: "Something from the deep woods, or something that shouldn't exist at all. The claws are wrong for any creature I know. It's heading toward the settlement."
"Good eyes. See there? The gait pattern shifts. It went from four legs to two. Whatever this thing is, it's not just an animal."
A ranger tends to a large hunting hawk perched on a gloved fist. A wolf lies at their feet. "The animals are restless. They sense what we cannot."

transitions.text: Can you help me befriend a wild animal? [INS 13]

states.text: "The birds have stopped singing near the ruins. The wolves avoid the eastern forest entirely. Animals know when the earth itself is sick. Something beneath is stirring."

transitions.text: Even nature knows something is wrong.

states.text: "You have a natural affinity. I can teach you the old ways of bonding with a forest creature. It takes patience and genuine respect for the wild."
A ranger lies propped against a tree, bloody bandages wrapped around one leg. "Don't come closer. There's something in the trees. It got me while I was scouting."
"Thank you. That salve's good work. I owe you my leg, maybe my life. The outpost is half a league south. Tell them what happened."
"Something between a wolf and a man. Moved on all fours then stood upright. Eyes like burning coals. It came from the direction of the ruins."

params.entry: A wounded ranger describes being attacked by a wolf-like creature that walks on two legs, coming from the ruins.

states.text: A noble with fine bearing studies you from across a well-appointed study. "I've been seeking capable individuals for a venture. Your reputation precedes you."
"The ancient ruins hold artifacts of immense value. I'll fund an expedition if you lead it. Whatever scholarly finds surface go to me. You keep the rest."

transitions.text: The ruins are too dangerous right now.

states.text: "Fifty gold advance, plus whatever loot you find that isn't of scholarly interest. And my gratitude, which opens doors in this city."
A calculating noble studies a chessboard, occasionally glancing up. "Every piece on this board has a purpose. Even the pawns. Tell me, what is your purpose here?"

transitions.text: I don't have time for games.

states.text: "Sharp mind. I could use someone who thinks before acting. The mayor is compromised, the merchant guild is arming, and the temple is hiding something. Choose a side wisely."
"The game of power, naturally. This town sits on something valuable, and everyone wants control. I intend to be the one who gets it."

transitions.text: At least you're honest about it.

states.text: A fallen noble in threadbare finery sits in a dingy tavern corner. "Once I had lands, titles, servants. Now I have this cup and whatever's left of my dignity."

transitions.text: Let me buy you a proper meal.

states.text: "Lord Castellan accused me of treason and seized my holdings. Forged evidence, bought witnesses. I fled before the executioner came. Everything I've told the authorities falls on deaf ears."

transitions.text: Castellan has enemies. You're not alone.

states.text: "Kind of you. Kindness is rare these days. If you ever need someone who knows the inner workings of the noble houses, find me. I may be down, but I'm not useless."

transitions.text: I may take you up on that.

states.text: A taciturn ferryman leans on his pole beside a flat-bottomed boat. "Three gold to cross. Five if you're in a hurry. No credit."

transitions.text: Seen anything strange on the river?

states.text: The ferryman pushes off from the bank with practiced ease. "Keep still and we'll make the other side in one piece. Probably."
"Bodies floating downstream three days back. And the water's turned a strange color near the cave outlet. Something foul upstream. I don't go that way anymore."
A grizzled deep-shaft miner drinks alone, hands still trembling. "Don't go down to the fourth level. I don't care what they're paying. It's not worth it."

transitions.text: Let me buy you another drink. Calm your nerves.
You look like you need rest.

states.text: "Walls that move when you're not looking. Sounds of breathing from the stone itself. And we broke through into a chamber that wasn't on any map. Symbols on the walls, like the ones at the ruins. We sealed it and ran."

transitions.text: The ruins reach this far underground?

params.entry: A miner found a sealed chamber on the fourth level with binding symbols matching the ancient ruins.

states.text: The miner takes a long drink and steadies himself. "Thanks. I needed that. If you're heading into the mines, talk to the foreman. And bring a weapon. A pickaxe won't cut it."
A fortune teller draped in shimmering veils beckons from behind a crystal orb. "Cross my palm with silver and I'll show you what fate has in store."
"I see a journey into darkness. A sealed door that must be opened. And a choice: power or sacrifice. The stars say you will face the Sleeper before the season turns."
"Most of it is show for the marks. But the crystal... sometimes it shows things I can't explain. Lately all it shows is darkness rising from below. That part is real, and it terrifies me."
The watchtower guard squints over the palisade. "State your business. The road's been ugly all week."

params.text: The watchtower guard needs lamp oil and bandages before nightfall.

states.text: "Scouts saw tracks circling the ridge trail. Too disciplined for wolves, too quiet for goblins. Somebody's testing our patrols."
"Good. Bring what you can before sundown and maybe we keep the signal fire burning."
A dust-caked caravaneer pats a ledger against one palm. "If you're buying, talk fast. If you're helping, talk faster."

transitions.text: You look short-handed. Need an escort?

states.text: "Road tolls are rising, marsh routes are drowning, and half the porters swear the north markers are moving on their own. Business is lively in all the worst ways."

transitions.text: That's enough bad news for one day.

states.text: "Good. Get this manifest to the river post and bring back their seal. I'll pay for proof, not promises."
The innkeeper leans in with a worried look. "If you're after stew, wait a minute. If you're after the noises under my cellar, we should talk now."

transitions.text: I'll look into the cellar for you.

states.text: "Scratching. Knocking. Sometimes a hymn, but sung backwards. Guests don't sleep much after hearing it. Neither do I."
"Bless you. If you clear it out, your room and supper are on the house."

transitions.text: I'll be back before last call.

states.text: The blacksmith rakes coals into the forge. "If you've brought ore, I can make steel. If you've brought excuses, take them outside."
"Orders from the guard, hinges for the chapel, nails for half the town, and barely enough iron to keep one hammer swinging. That's how work is."
"Bring me sound ore and I'll settle the rest in coin and a proper edge."

transitions.text: I'll see what I can find.

params.text: The blacksmith needs usable ore to keep the watch armed.

states.text: A field healer sorts wrapped herbs beside a travel cot. "Wounded? Poisoned? Or just curious why the triage tent is still full?"

transitions.text: Take this coin for bandages and broth.

states.text: "Not plague, thank the light. Cuts, marsh fever, a few broken ribs, and too many guards pretending they'll be fine by dawn."

transitions.text: I'll keep out of your way.

states.text: "That will help more than you know. Here—take a restorative before someone worse off needs it."

transitions.text: Use it well? I intend to.

states.text: A hooded fixer cracks the safehouse door just wide enough to study you. "Password, payment, or a very good reason. Pick one."

transitions.text: I can move a message unseen.

states.text: "Not cargo. Names. Routes. Which guards look away, which captains sell what they seize, which priests drink with the wrong nobles. That's the real trade."

transitions.text: That's enough truth for one alley.

states.text: "Good. Carry this cipher sheet to the lantern post and wait for a blue spark. If you see red, run."
A road-weary refugee clutches a blanket close. "If you're with the tax men, I've got nothing. If you're not, maybe you've got a moment."

transitions.text: I can help you recover what was taken.

states.text: "We left before dawn when the smoke started. By noon the wagons were ash and the tally books were gone. Without the ledger, the quartermaster says we don't exist."

transitions.text: That's cruel even by frontier standards.

states.text: "Find the ledger and the children eat this week. That's all I can offer—except thanks."
A cult herald smiles as if greeting an honored guest. "You are late, but not unwelcome. The fire still answers your footsteps."

transitions.text: What fire are you talking about?
Tell me where the signal site is.

states.text: "Three fires wake the old road: one for the sleepers below, one for the oathbreakers above, and one for those foolish enough to answer."

transitions.text: You speak in riddles on purpose.

states.text: "At the ridge beacon. Follow the ash, not the road. If you reach the stones before moonrise, you may yet stop what comes next."
A cartographer weighted down with map tubes taps a blank spot on a chart. "Road's there. Hills are there. Beacon ruin is not there, and that's the problem."
"Because every smuggler, cultist, and militia captain in three counties keeps pretending it isn't there. When maps lie, people are usually paid to make them lie."
"Bring back distances, watch posts, and any carvings on the ruin stones. I pay for useful lines, not pretty ones."

transitions.text: I'll return with a proper survey.

params.text: The cartographer needs the ridge beacon mapped before smugglers claim it.

states.text: The harbormaster-mayor glances from tide charts to militia rosters. "Dock fees, storm damage, missing crates, missing people. Welcome to leadership."

transitions.text: The beacon route is charted and secure.

states.text: "Truth? Fear. Storms pass. Broken piers get rebuilt. But when folk believe the coast road belongs to raiders after dusk, trade dies before the first sail tears."

transitions.text: Then I'll help kill the rumor.

states.text: "Good. The patrol captains can use your map, and the port can breathe again for a week or two. You've earned this."

A sage surrounded by glowing crystals and half-finished enchantments looks up from a worktable covered in arcane dust. "You seek to imbue the mundane with the arcane? That takes knowledge and rare materials."

transitions.text: I have an arcane crystal. Can you teach me enchanting?
I need to identify an enchanted weapon.

states.text: "To enchant a weapon, you need a base blade from a skilled smith, arcane crystals from deep caverns, and elemental essences drawn from the planes. The forge and the alchemy bench are your two workstations. Start simple — a dagger or longsword — before attempting elemental blades."

transitions.text: What else can you tell me?
Good to know. I will prepare.

params.text: The arcane sage explained the basics of enchanting: base weapons, arcane crystals, and elemental essences are the key components.

states.text: "The crystal resonates with your intent. Remember: moonstone dust stabilizes the enchantment on blades. Fire and water essences shape elemental properties. Storm crystals are the rarest — save them for maces and staves."

params.text: The arcane sage taught me the fundamentals of weapon enchanting. Moonstone dust stabilizes enchantments, elemental essences shape them.

states.text: "This blade carries a binding ward from the old order. The enchantment strengthens when the wielder focuses their will. It will serve you well."
A witch tends a garden of luminous herbs behind her cottage, crystalline vials arranged on shelves. "I trade in essences. Bring me what the wilds provide, and I will give you what the forge demands."

transitions.text: I have a troll heart to trade.
I have dragon blood to trade.

states.text: "Troll hearts yield earth essence. Dragon blood distills into fire essence. Venom sacs produce necrotic essence. Phoenix ash is too precious to trade — keep what you find for your own alchemy."

transitions.text: Let me check what I have.
I will gather what I can.

states.text: "The heart pulses with raw elemental force. Here — two vials of earth essence, refined and ready for the forge."
"Dragon blood burns even in glass. Three vials of fire essence. Use them wisely — flametongue blades drink this like water."

transitions.text: I will put them to use.

states.text: A priest kneels before a radiant altar, candlelight reflecting off polished silver vessels. "The light can strengthen what mortal hands forge. Do you carry something in need of blessing?"

transitions.text: I need help purifying a necrotic essence.

states.text: "Moonstone dust, when consecrated, becomes a powerful stabilizer for enchantments. Bring your reagents before crafting, and I will ask the light to strengthen them. The forge responds to blessed materials with greater potency."

transitions.text: I will return when I have materials.

states.text: "The darkness in this essence resists, but the light prevails. Here — an ethereal essence, cleansed and ready for noble craft. Use it to forge rings or imbue staves with otherworldly power."

params.text: The priest purified a necrotic essence into ethereal essence. Ethereal essence is used in high-tier enchantments like the Ring of the Arcane.

states.text: "Warding rings and enchanted shields draw their power from protective glyphs. Silver is the ideal metal — it channels moonstone dust better than iron. A ring of warding is the simplest ward to forge, requiring only silver ore, moonstone dust, and an arcane crystal."
An experienced healer examines rows of labeled vials, comparing the color of two liquids against candlelight. "Most healers brew what they need. I study what is possible. Are you here to learn?"

transitions.text: I found phoenix ash in the mountains.
Do you know where to find rare reagents?

states.text: "Greater healing potions require phoenix ash — the rebirth residue is what makes them restore so much vitality. Superior potions add dragon blood for sustained regeneration. Both demand an alembic as well as a mortar. The alchemy bench is essential."

transitions.text: Where can I find these ingredients?

states.text: "Phoenix ash! This is extraordinarily rare. I can brew two greater healing potions from this single pinch. Take them — you earned them, and here is gold for your trouble."

"Phoenix ash falls from the nests in the high peaks. Dragon blood pools in the lairs east of the marshlands. Arcane crystals grow deep in the old mines. The elemental wards in the dwarven hold are the richest source of elemental essences I know of."

params.text: The healer revealed reagent locations: phoenix ash in the high peaks, dragon blood in eastern lairs, arcane crystals in old mines, elemental essences in the dwarven hold.

states.text: A blacksmith works at a forge whose flames burn an unnatural blue. Runes glow on the anvil. "I forge what others cannot. Bring me the right materials and I will make the extraordinary."

transitions.text: I want a flametongue blade. I have the materials.

params.text: The enchanter blacksmith confirmed he can forge elemental weapons if I bring the right materials.

transitions.text: I need storm crystals for a thunderstrike mace.

params.text: The enchanter smith needs storm crystals from the elemental ward chambers for a thunderstrike mace. I should seek out the crystal caverns and the essence wellspring.

states.text: "Flametongue blades need fire essence and blazing crystals. Frostbrand swords need water essence and tidal crystals. The thunderstrike mace requires storm crystals, storm essence, and mithral. All start with a base weapon you have already forged. Bring me the components and I will show you how."

transitions.text: How do I find these materials?
I will gather what I need.

states.text: "Fire essence, good. You will also need a blazing crystal and a finished steel sword. Forge the base blade yourself or buy one, then bring everything to an enchanting forge. The runes on the anvil will guide the essence into the steel."

"Storm crystals form only where elemental air and water clash. The dwarven hold's elemental wards — the place they call the Dual Ward Chamber — is your best chance. The crystal caverns beyond the peaks also hold untapped veins."
A merchant displays wares on a cloth embroidered with arcane sigils. Crystal vials, essence bottles, and enchanted threads catch the light. "Magic has its price. What do you need?"

transitions.text: I am a regular buyer. Can we negotiate a discount?
I want to sell enchanted items.

states.text: "I stock arcane crystals, moonstone dust, elemental dust, mystic ink, parchment, and glass vials. For rarer essences — fire, water, storm — you will need to gather those yourself or trade with the witches in the wilds."
"A savvy buyer. Very well — ten percent off reagents for the rest of the day. I know a fellow crafter when I see one."
"Enchanted goods always find buyers among the caravan masters and noble houses. Here is a fair price for what you have brought."
A sage carefully inscribes glowing runes onto parchment, tongue between teeth. "Silence, please. One wrong stroke and this scroll becomes a fireball in my lap."

transitions.text: Tell me how scrolls are made.
I have mystic ink for your research.
Can you write a scroll for me?

states.text: "Scroll writing is alchemy with ink. You need parchment, mystic ink, and a component matching the spell — bat guano and sulfur for fireball, storm essence for lightning. A workbench is sufficient. Precision matters more than strength."

transitions.text: Can I buy a scroll from you?
I will try writing one myself.

params.text: The sage explained scroll crafting: parchment, mystic ink, and spell components at a workbench. Bat guano and sulfur for fireball scrolls.

states.text: "Two bottles of mystic ink. Excellent. Take this fireball scroll — consider it payment for your contribution to my research. The formulas I am developing will require far more."
"One hundred gold and I will inscribe a lightning bolt scroll. Fair warning — this one packs a real punch. Do not unroll it near anything flammable."
A witch works over a copper still, channeling colored vapors into glass vessels. "The trick is knowing which things want to become other things. Bring me the raw, I give you the refined."

transitions.text: I have venom sacs to trade.
I have spider silk for enchanted thread.

states.text: "Two venom sacs yield one necrotic essence. Spider silk, when treated with moonstone dust, becomes enchanted thread — essential for enchanted armor. Ectoplasm refines into ethereal essence, but that requires a full moon and more patience than most possess."

transitions.text: Let me check what I have.

states.text: "Potent. One vial of necrotic essence, distilled and sealed. The priests can purify this into ethereal essence if you need it for warding enchantments."
"Spider silk holds enchantment better than any mortal thread. One spool of enchanted thread, ready for the forge. Use it to weave arcane protection into chain or plate armor."
A healer with bloodstained sleeves looks up from a workbench covered in rolled bandages and jars of salve. "Wounds do not wait for manners. Show me what hurts or tell me what you need."

transitions.text: I brought torn cloth for bandages.
How do you make healing salve?

states.text: "Clean the wound first — spring water or antiseptic tincture. Pack it with healing salve to stop infection. Then wrap it tight with bandages. Field bandages work in a pinch, but proper linen dressings hold better. Change them twice a day."

params.text: The healer taught wound care basics: clean with antiseptic, apply healing salve, wrap with field bandages. Change dressings twice daily.

states.text: "Cloth is always in short supply. Three lengths will make a dozen bandages — enough for the evening rush. Take this healing potion as thanks. We look after those who look after us."

"Healing salve is the simplest medicine worth making. Crush healing herbs with a mortar, mix in honey for binding, and spread it on torn cloth strips. Any alchemy bench will do. Even a novice can manage it at difficulty ten."

A field medic crouches beside an open kit of tinctures and splints, hands still dusted with dried blood. "Triage is about order. You treat the dying first, the broken second, and the bruised when there is time. What do you need?"

transitions.text: I have healing herbs for the wounded.

states.text: "Bleeding comes first — field bandages or bloodclot poultice. Broken bones need splint braces before the patient can be moved. Infections need antiseptic tincture within the hour or the wound turns septic. Pain suppressors help, but use them sparingly — ghostcap numbs the mind as well as the body."

params.text: The field medic explained triage priorities: bleeding first (bandages, poultice), broken bones (splint braces), infection (antiseptic tincture), and pain management (pain suppressor).

states.text: "Two bundles of healing herbs. That will keep the worst cases stable until proper medicine arrives. Here — the camp fund can spare this much for your generosity."

"Surgery demands a surgical kit — clean blades, needles, thread, vials for drainage. You can assemble one at a workbench from leather, iron nails, torn cloth, and glass vials. It is leatherworking, not alchemy, but the precision required is just as demanding."

A priest stands over a row of cots in a makeshift ward, incense burning against the stench of fever. "The plague does not care about prayers alone. We need medicine, clean water, and hands willing to help. Are you here to serve?"

transitions.text: I brought mandrake root for the antidote.
I will find the source of this plague.

params.text: The priest in the plague ward asked me to find the source of the infection. The mining camp's water supply may be poisoned.

states.text: "It began three weeks ago in the mining camp. Fever, then sores, then the lungs fill. Mandrake root and moonpetal brewed together at an alchemy bench make the antidote — but the roots are scarce and the miners cannot leave their cots to gather them. Someone poisoned the well. I am certain of it."

transitions.text: I will search for the source.

states.text: "Mandrake root — the gods have answered. This single root can save three lives if brewed correctly. The people of this ward will remember your name. You have earned their respect and mine."

"The poisoned wellspring lies beneath the mining tunnels. Plague rats guard the approach. Be careful — the water itself is toxic. Bring plague samples back to any alchemist and they can confirm the source. Then purify the well."

A priest sits in a quiet chapel adjoining the infirmary, sacred oils and blessed cloths arranged on a small altar. "Recovery is more than mending flesh. The spirit needs rest as much as the body needs bandages. How may I help you heal?"

transitions.text: Tell me about recovery and rest.
Bless me for the road ahead.
Thank you, I feel better already.

states.text: "Rest is the foundation. A clean bed, warm broth, and time. Medicinal broth speeds the body's natural recovery — herb heal simmered with salt at a campfire. Ironbark tonic fortifies endurance for those who cannot afford to rest. But nothing replaces sleep and stillness."
"May the light mend what is broken and guard what remains whole. Go with renewed strength. This blessing will ease your recovery should you fall in battle."

"Infection is the silent killer after every battle. Antiseptic tincture — wolfsbane distilled with spring water — must be applied within the hour. After that, bloodclot poultice to seal the wound and healing salve to draw out any remaining poison. The alchemist's bench is your best friend against infection."

transitions.text: Tell me about rest and recovery.

params.text: The priest explained infection treatment: antiseptic tincture within the hour, bloodclot poultice to seal, healing salve to draw out poison. All crafted at an alchemy bench.

states.text: A sage pores over anatomical diagrams and herbal texts, a human skeleton hanging from a stand beside the desk. "Medicine is a science, not a prayer. Understanding the body's structure is the first step to mending it. What brings you to my study?"

transitions.text: I have plague essence for your research.
What do the texts say about herbal remedies?
I will leave you to your studies.

states.text: "Surgery demands precision. A surgical kit — assembled from leather, iron nails, clean cloth, and glass vials at a workbench — contains the essential tools: scalpels, clamps, sutures. The leatherworking skill translates well to surgical assembly. Difficulty sixteen, for those skilled enough."

params.text: The medical sage described surgical kit assembly: leather, iron nails, torn cloth, and glass vials at a workbench using leatherworking skill at DC 16.

states.text: "Plague essence — dangerous to handle, invaluable for study. I can isolate the pathogen and develop a countermeasure. Here is payment for the risk you took in collecting it. This will advance our understanding enormously."

"The ancients knew their herbs. Bloodthorn for clotting, chamomile for fever, ironwood bark for fortitude, ghostcap for pain. Mandrake root is the keystone of plague treatment — combined with moonpetal at an alchemy bench, it creates the only reliable plague antidote known. Difficulty sixteen, and worth every effort."

A sage tends a small indoor garden of medicinal plants, each labeled with precise notation. "Every herb in this garden can save a life or take one, depending on the dosage. Shall I teach you the difference?"

transitions.text: I found moonpetal in the wild. What is it worth?

states.text: "Healing herbs are common and versatile — salves, potions, even teas. Wolfsbane is a powerful antiseptic when diluted properly, lethal if ingested raw. Nightshade numbs pain but clouds the mind. Mandrake root is the rarest — it only grows where the dead have rested, and it is the only cure for the southern plague."

transitions.text: I will gather what I can.

states.text: "Moonpetal — it blooms only under a full moon and wilts by dawn. Combined with mandrake root, it creates the plague antidote. Alone, it soothes fever and eases breathing. Thirty-five gold is a fair price for one this fresh."

"Antiseptic tincture requires wolfsbane — yes, the poison — diluted with spring water and sealed in a glass vial. At an alchemy bench, difficulty twelve. The key is concentration: too strong and it burns healthy flesh, too weak and the infection persists. A mortar is essential for grinding the wolfsbane to the right consistency."

params.text: The herbalist sage explained antiseptic tincture: wolfsbane diluted with spring water in a glass vial, brewed at an alchemy bench at DC 12 using a mortar.

states.text: A merchant stands behind crates stamped with a red cross, shelves stocked with bandage rolls, herb bundles, and glass vials. "Medical supplies. Steady demand, slim margins, and the satisfaction of knowing your goods keep people breathing. What do you need?"

transitions.text: I have medical supplies to sell.
I need supplies for a field hospital. Can you discount?

states.text: "Healing herbs, torn cloth for bandages, mandrake root when I can get it, glass vials, wolfsbane in small doses. I also stock honey — healers swear by it for wound dressings. Moonpetal is seasonal. For plague antidote ingredients, you will need both mandrake root and moonpetal together."

transitions.text: I will take what I need.

states.text: "Medical goods always have a buyer. The healers' guild, the military quartermasters, the temples — everyone needs bandages and salves. Here is a fair price for what you have brought."
"A field hospital? You must be headed somewhere dangerous. Very well — fifteen percent off the lot for bulk medical orders. I will set aside extra bandage cloth and healing herbs for you."

An apothecary arranges rows of labeled tinctures and stoppered vials behind a polished counter. The air smells of chamomile and alcohol. "Remedies, tinctures, and tonics. I stock what the healers prescribe and the soldiers demand. Name your ailment."

transitions.text: I need an antidote for poison.
What do soldiers take for recovery after battle?

states.text: "Healing salve for wounds, antiseptic tincture for infection, fever tea for the burning, pain suppressors for the worst of it, and ironbark tonic for those who need endurance in the field. For plague cases, only the plague antidote will do — mandrake root and moonpetal, brewed at an alchemy bench."

transitions.text: I know what I need now.

states.text: "Poison is nothing to trifle with. These healing herbs can be brewed into a proper antidote at any alchemy bench — herb cure, honey, and a glass vial. Or eat them raw for a lesser effect. Twenty-five gold for the pair."

"The garrison buys medicinal broth by the barrel — herb heal, salt, and water simmered at a campfire. Ironbark tonic for the officers who cannot afford a day in bed. Restorative stew for the ones strong enough to eat solid food again. Field bandages and splint braces round out any field kit."

params.text: The apothecary listed recovery essentials: medicinal broth (campfire), ironbark tonic and antiseptic tincture (alchemy bench), restorative stew (kitchen), field bandages and splint braces (workbench).

states.text: A battlefield mentor taps a practice blade against a shield boss. "Second wind is not magic. It is breathing, footwork, and refusing to break when the line wavers. Want the drill?"

params.text: The warrior mentor taught me that second wind comes from breath control, stance, and never surrendering the line.

states.text: "When the line breaks, do not chase glory. Plant your feet, slow your breath, and let the enemy overextend. Second Wind is for the moment when the foe thinks you are done."
"Run the guard steps again. Breathe in on the advance, out on the brace, and never leave your flank exposed. A warrior who recovers well can rejoin the fight before the enemy notices the gap."

A scarred veteran studies the parade yard and points at the painted lines in the dirt. "Heroes rush. Survivors keep discipline. If your companions are going to fight like a unit, they need rules. Ready for the lesson?"

transitions.text: How do guard and aggressive tactics differ?

states.text: "Hold the center. Keep the shield wall tidy. If a companion breaks formation to chase a kill, call them back. Guard mode protects the leader; aggressive mode chases openings; balanced mode keeps the line intact."

transitions.text: I will keep the line intact.

params.text: The veteran taught line discipline: hold center, protect the leader, and only break formation when the plan demands it.

states.text: "Balanced is the steady march. Guard keeps one eye on threats to the player and formation. Aggressive hunts the weakest enemy and closes fast. A disciplined party swaps modes without losing the shape of the line."

A guard captain stands over a chalkboard of marching arrows and shield positions. "Formation is the difference between a patrol and a panic. I teach the watch to protect the commander and never crowd the weak side."

transitions.text: How should companions behave in guard mode?

params.text: The guard captain explained that guard mode keeps allies near the leader, intercepts threats, and avoids reckless pursuit.

states.text: "Formation keeps movement predictable. Wedge pushes hard, line holds ground, and spread gives room to breathe. If you order aggressive behavior, you must still respect spacing or your own allies will block the advance."
"Guard mode means the nearest threat to the commander gets answered first. No sprinting past danger. No chasing a dying foe when the flank is open. Keep the shield between the party and the enemy."
A rogue mentor leans in the alley shade, one eye on the rooftops and one on the street. "A rogue wins by choosing when to move, not by moving first. Do you want the lesson in patience or pursuit?"

transitions.text: How does aggressive tactics work for a rogue?
I will practice in the field.

states.text: "Balanced keeps you alive, guard keeps you near the pack, and aggressive only works when the enemy is already off-balance. Strike the weak side, not the obvious one."

transitions.text: I will keep my head down.

states.text: "If you go aggressive, do not rush the front. Circle to a flank, hit the soft target, and leave before the enemy can pin you. A rogue that breaks formation without purpose dies alone."

params.text: The rogue mentor taught me to favor flanks and never to abandon the team just to chase a kill.

states.text: A scout kneels beside a map marked with trail signs and escape routes. "The best scouts are the ones the enemy never realizes are there. Want to learn how to keep pace without breaking discipline?"

transitions.text: How do I drill companion flanks?

params.text: The scout showed me how to keep formation discipline while using the edges of the fight to flank safely.

states.text: "A scout watches the field. Balanced is for moving with the team. Guard is for keeping the commander alive. Aggressive is for turning an opening into a collapse, but only when the path is clear."

transitions.text: I will keep the path clear.

states.text: "The drill is simple: move only when the enemy's attention shifts, keep the line tight, and never outrun your allies. A scout who lives long enough to report back is more valuable than a scout who boasts about a kill."

A fence flicks a coin across their knuckles and watches the door instead of your face. "I deal in quiet favors. Discipline keeps a crew alive; careless aggression gets the watch involved. Need the backroom rules?"

transitions.text: What does a fence think of guard and aggressive tactics?

states.text: "Guard mode is for keeping the boss alive. Aggressive is for making a scene. Balanced is what you choose when you want everyone to walk out without a story attached to the knives."

transitions.text: I will keep my hands steady.

params.text: The fence reminded me that discipline matters more than swagger when the crew needs to leave without attracting trouble.

states.text: "A disciplined crew does not charge every fight. Let the guard hold the line, let the aggressive cut a path when it matters, and let balanced keep everyone where they can still make the escape."
A mage mentor is surrounded by chalk circles and half-burned spell notes. "Arcane recovery is not about having more power. It is about wasting less. Do you want the lesson in spell conservation?"

transitions.text: How do I conserve spell points?
I will return to the books.

states.text: "Save your second tier spells for when they matter. Arcane Recovery works best after a long rest because your mind needs time to settle. The more carefully you cast, the further your spell points will carry you."

"Cast for purpose, not flourish. A mage who counts every spell point can survive a day that would exhaust a show-off before sunset. That is how recovery becomes a strategy instead of a desperation move."

params.text: The mage mentor taught me that arcane recovery rewards restraint. Save spell points, rest well, and spend magic only when it changes the fight.

states.text: A sage traces circles in the dust around a stack of spellbooks. "Students think more spells mean more wisdom. I think wisdom means knowing when not to spend one. Need a lesson in conservation?"

transitions.text: How does arcane recovery actually work?

params.text: The sage explained that recovery is about pacing, long rests, and reserving enough spell points to answer the unexpected.

states.text: "A wizard who empties the mind too quickly is no wizard at all. Keep one spell in reserve for the door you did not see coming, and one for the ally who needs you to stand your ground."

"The body needs sleep and the mind needs stillness. Arcane recovery is what happens when both agree to stop arguing for a while. Spend sparingly, rest deliberately, and the spell points return with less friction."

A priest mentor arranges candles around a small prayer circle. "Divinity is not for show. Channel it when the faithful are in need, and save greater miracles for the fight that demands them. Ready for instruction?"

transitions.text: When should I use greater heal?

states.text: "Channel divinity is for the moment when the whole line needs a blessing or a reprieve. It is not a trick to waste on a scratch. Use it when your companions are committed and the battle would turn if they held just a little longer."

transitions.text: I will save it for the line.

states.text: "Greater heal is a vow made useful. It asks a priest to spend real power to pull a companion back from the edge. Keep enough spell points in reserve, because miracles are expensive and the wounded rarely give warning."

params.text: The priest mentor taught me to reserve channel divinity for the decisive moment and greater heal for the ally the fight would otherwise take.

states.text: A healer folds fresh linen into neat squares beside a row of broth pots. "Aftercare keeps a victory from becoming a funeral later. Want the lesson in rest, bandages, and the long road back?"

params.text: The healer explained that aftercare is a blend of rest, clean dressings, warm broth, and watching for infection before it spreads.

transitions.text: I will come back with supplies.

states.text: "A battle does not end when the last enemy falls. It ends when the wounds are cleaned, the fever is gone, and the body has time to mend. Recovery is a discipline too, even if it is a quieter one."

"Bandages, broth, and patience. If a companion is hurt badly enough to need greater heal, do not wait until they collapse. Make the call early and keep them alive long enough for the miracle to matter."

A shrine mentor kneels before a stone altar lined with votive candles. "The shrine teaches patience, and patience teaches power. Greater heal is sacred, but so is restraint. Shall I show you the rite?"

transitions.text: How does the greater heal rite work?
I will return after I pray.
